content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration can be reframed to chunk environmental information into semantically coherent 3–5 bounded working memory chunks aligned to human-like working memory capacity limits, turning the inherent constraint of limited working memory into a feature that avoids information overload for autonomous agents. This creates a curiosity search space that prioritizes exploring novel, high-information chunks instead of randomly sampling trivial low-value states.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
